title: Sopa de Tortilla (Tortilla Soup)

overview.description: A traditional Mexican chicken and tomato soup finished with crispy tortilla strips, avocado, cheese and fresh lime.

method: Heat the oil over a medium heat.
Cook the onion for 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and jalapeños and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, oregano and smoked paprika.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes.
Pour in the homemade chicken stock.
Blend until smooth if preferred, or leave rustic with some texture.
Add the shredded chicken and black beans, if using.
Season to taste and stir in the lime juice.
Meanwhile, cut the tortillas into thin strips.
Brush lightly with oil and bake at 200°C (180°C fan) for 8–10 minutes until crisp.
Top with the tortilla strips, avocado, cheese and fresh coriander.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional tortilla soup is believed to have originated in central Mexico and uses stale tortillas to add flavour and texture rather than wasting them.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a tomato-based broth, prepare crispy tortilla strips and layer fresh toppings for texture and freshness.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fresh, vibrant soup that makes excellent use of homemade tortillas, homemade chicken stock and leftover cooked chicken.
chapman-tips: Homemade corn tortillas make noticeably better tortilla strips than shop-bought wraps.
Bake the tortilla strips rather than frying for a lighter finish.
Add the toppings immediately before serving so they stay fresh and crisp.
Blend the soup or leave it chunky depending on your preferred texture.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the soup base only. Add fresh toppings after reheating.
notes.leftovers: Excellent the following day with freshly baked tortilla strips.
